text: High-performance deep learning (DLC) models are constantly producing state-of-the-art results in computer vision and natural language processing (NLP) and many other applications. With the progress in high-performance computing and advanced training resources, high-performance DLC models are usually of high complexity, which leads to huge model parameters, long inference latency, and large training resources. As a result, model efficiency is as important as the model performance. In this survey, we investigate and summarize the efficient deep learning techniques along five aspects, i.e., model compression, learning, automation, network architecture design, and infrastructure (including hardware and software). Also, we give a full picture of the fundamental works in quantization, pruning, knowledge distillation and neural architecture search (NAS). A mental model is proposed to assist practitioners to decide which efficient deep learning approaches should be applied to their own models at which stage of their development, namely at "shrink-and-improve." The latter is supported by an experiment guide with corresponding open-source code. To the best of our knowledge, our work is the first survey that explores the efficient deep insights into the infrastructure for model efficiency, spanning all the way from the modeling aspects down to the hardware supports, which would largely facilitate the development of the future efficient deep learning systems for researchers and practitioners.
explanation: The text reads like a polished survey abstract with broad, generic claims and few concrete details, which is common in AI-generated writing. Some phrasing is slightly awkward or generic (e.g., 'DLC' and 'shrink-and-improve'), suggesting it may have been produced by AI, though a human-written abstract is also possible.
